explainability.findings: The company's privacy policy mentions automated decision-making. It references the right to object to these decisions, human review of automated outputs, and the ability to challenge decisions.
explainability.path_to_improvement: Publish user-facing explanation interfaces or documented appeal workflows.

relevant_sources.summary: The Molson Coors Privacy Policy provides evidence for **privacy, governance, oversight, and explainability**. This policy document supports the **privacy** pillar by detailing data sharing practices for profiling and targeted advertising, and by acknowledging consent for automated data collection. It demonstrates **governance** through mechanisms for user control over data sharing related to profiling and by outlining policies for data handling with third parties. Evidence for **oversight** and **explainability** is found in the policy's mention of automated decision-making, the right to object, human review of automated outputs, and the ability to challenge decisions.
relevant_sources.title: Molson Coors Privacy Policy

external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Obtain external validation of AI practices or require vendor certifications.

fairness.path_to_improvement: Document bias testing procedures or vendor AI fairness requirements.

governance.findings: An SEC filing indicates the company's awareness of AI integration and associated risks, suggesting the implementation of an AI governance program to manage these challenges, particularly concerning cybersecurity. Additionally, a privacy policy outlines policies for data handling with third parties and demonstrates governance through mechanisms for user control over data sharing related to profiling.
governance.path_to_improvement: Name an AI governance body with defined mandate covering all AI use.

relevant_sources.summary: The Molson Coors 2024 10-K Annual Report (SEC Filing) provides evidence for the **governance** pillar of responsible AI. This SEC filing indicates the company's awareness of AI integration and the associated risks, suggesting the implementation of an AI governance program to manage these challenges, particularly concerning cybersecurity. The report's mention of operating such a program, while lacking specific execution details, aligns with the identification of policy-level statements regarding AI governance.
relevant_sources.title: Molson Coors 2024 10-K Annual Report (SEC Filing)

transparency.findings: A blog post from Atwater Brewery details the AI's role and capabilities in developing a beer recipe. It explicitly mentions the use of an AI chatbot and its functions in this product development process.
transparency.path_to_improvement: Document AI systems in use, including vendor/third-party AI tools.

relevant_sources.summary: This blog post from Molson Coors' Atwater Brewery provides evidence for the **transparency** pillar of responsible AI. It details the AI's role and capabilities in developing a beer recipe, explicitly mentioning the use of an AI chatbot and its functions in the process. This documentation demonstrates transparency regarding the AI's application in product development.
relevant_sources.title: Atwater Brews Beer Developed by AI (Molson Coors Blog)

summary.overall_findings: Molson Coors Beverage Company's privacy policy details data sharing practices for profiling and targeted advertising, while its governance materials indicate awareness of AI integration and associated risks. Explainability, oversight, privacy, and governance are addressed at the policy level, accounting for 4 of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars with documented evidence. For instance, a privacy policy also acknowledges consent for automated data collection and references the right to object to automated decisions. No qualifying public evidence was found for transparency, fairness, and 1 additional pillar. This assessment draws on 11 publicly available sources.
